# Test mcp-probe + mcp-shadow + mcpdiff integration
# This integration test verifies the tools work together correctly

# Build all the required tools
exec go build -o probe ../../../../cmd/mcp-probe/main.go
exec go build -o shadow ../../../../cmd/mcp-shadow
exec go build -o diff ../../../../cmd/mcpdiff
exec go build -o server ../../../../cmd/mcp-probe/minimal_server.go

# Test 1: Get sample requests from mcp-probe
exec ./probe
stdout '{"ID":{},"Method":"initialize"'
stdout '{"ID":{},"Method":"tools/call"'

# Test 2: Run a simple echo through shadow
exec sh -c 'echo ''{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"test","params":{}}'' | ./shadow -primary "cat" -shadow "cat" -o trace.mcp -compare'
exec test -f trace.mcp

# Test 3: Test with actual servers
# Create a simple request
exec sh -c 'echo ''{"ID":{},"Method":"initialize","Params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26"}}'' > request.json'

# Run through shadow with servers
exec sh -c 'cat request.json | ./shadow -primary "./server" -shadow "./server" -o server-trace.mcp -timeout 3s -compare -v'
stdout 'Started primary and shadow servers'

# Test 4: Test mcpdiff with the trace
# Since mcpdiff needs two files, create a second trace
exec cp trace.mcp trace2.mcp
exec ./diff trace.mcp trace2.mcp
! stderr 'error:'

# Test 5: Full pipeline
exec sh -c './probe | head -1 | ./shadow -primary "./server" -shadow "./server" -o pipeline.mcp -timeout 2s -v'
exec test -f pipeline.mcp

# Test 6: Compare mode validation
exec ./diff --help
stdout 'Usage:'

# Cleanup
exec rm -f probe shadow diff server *.mcp *.json
